event: Christ the Ancient of Days Enthroned
symbols: Christ enthroned in a mandorla — the divine glory and sovereignty of Christ as eternal ruler
red seraphim flanking — the six-winged angels of Isaiah's vision crying 'Holy, holy, holy'
gold background — the uncreated light of divine eternity
Ancient of Days title — the identification of Christ with Daniel's vision of the eternal God
description: A small detail from a Russian Orthodox icon showing Christ enthroned within a dark blue mandorla or oval glory, set against a gold background. Christ is depicted as a young man with a halo, seated and holding a book or scroll. On either side, red six-winged seraphim flank the enthroned figure. The icon is painted in the traditional Byzantine style with gold leaf ground. The surface shows age and wear. The image appears as a semicircular or lunette detail from a larger icon, likely the Annunciation of Ustyug.
theological_description: The image of Christ as the Ancient of Days combines two scriptural visions. Daniel's vision describes 'one like a son of man' coming to the 'Ancient of Days' whose 'clothing was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool' (DAN.7.9-14). In Orthodox iconographic tradition, Christ himself is identified as the Ancient of Days, affirming his eternal divine nature — he who said 'Before Abraham was, I am' (JHN.8.58). The flanking seraphim recall Isaiah's temple vision: 'I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up... Above him stood the seraphim. Each had six wings... And one called to another and said: Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts' (ISA.6.1-3). John's Gospel identifies the Lord whom Isaiah saw as Christ himself (JHN.12.41). The mandorla of glory represents the uncreated divine light, the same glory that shone at the Transfiguration (MAT.17.2). As a detail from the Annunciation icon, this image declares that the child to be born of Mary is none other than the eternal God himself.

scripture_refs.reason: The Ancient of Days vision, the direct iconographic source for this depiction of Christ
Isaiah's vision of the Lord enthroned with seraphim, depicted here flanking Christ
Before Abraham was, I AM — Christ's claim to eternal preexistence as the Ancient of Days
Isaiah saw Christ's glory and spoke of him, identifying the Lord of Isaiah 6 with Jesus
The living creatures crying Holy, holy, holy, echoing the seraphim in Isaiah
